title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
About Miror

At Miror, we're on a mission to redefine how women experience midlife — especially during perimenopause, menopause, and beyond. This is a stage that affects every woman, bringing with it more than 50 physical and emotional symptoms that often go unaddressed or misunderstood. For too long, menopause has been surrounded by silence, stigma, and resignation.

Miror exists to change that — through science, technology, and empathy. We're building a FemTech ecosystem where women can access care, community, evidence-backed nutraceutical products, and credible information during one of the most transformative phases of life. Our mission is simple but bold: to make midlife a time to celebrate, not fear.

Our work so far

Over the last two years, Miror has become one of India's fastest-growing platforms for women's health and wellness. We've:

*   Partnered with experts from the Indian Menopause Society, the Government of Karnataka, and the National Health Mission
*   Built India's largest digital menopause care community, impacting over 80,000 women through online and offline initiatives
*   Been featured in Forbes, The Guardian, Times of India, Business Standard, and YourStory
*   Launched and scaled our technology platform — including the Miror app, Symptom Tracker, and a network of 100+ specialist doctors
*   Built a growing line of expertly formulated nutraceutical supplements designed for women in midlife
*   Launched industry-first programs: **Tiara** (India's first rewards program for women in midlife), the **HRT Centre of Excellence**, **All About You**, and **Workplace with Miror**

We're bootstrapped, revenue-generating, and growing on the strength of real users and real outcomes.

The opportunity

We're now entering our next phase of growth, and we're looking for an Operating CEO to run the company day-to-day alongside our Founder & Chairperson. The Founder will continue to drive long-term vision, brand, and ecosystem partnerships; you will own execution across the business — scaling our two core pillars of **technology** (app, digital platform, AI, care delivery) and **nutraceuticals** (formulation, manufacturing, supply chain, D2C commerce), alongside our services and community.

This is a mission-driven role that blends purpose, performance, and empathy — for a leader who knows how to operate a multi-product, bootstrapped business with discipline and ambition.

What you'll do

**Run the business**

*   Own the company P&L, operating plan, and quarterly execution across tech, nutra, services, and community
*   Drive sustainable, capital-efficient growth — we are bootstrapped and funded by revenue, not external capital
*   Set and manage budgets, unit economics, and operating KPIs across all business lines

**Scale the two core pillars**

*   **Technology:** partner with the CTPO to evolve the Miror app, digital platform, AI, and care delivery infrastructure into a category-defining FemTech product
*   **Nutraceuticals:** own the supplements business end-to-end — product portfolio, R&D and formulation partnerships, manufacturing and supply chain, regulatory compliance (FSSAI, AYUSH, labelling), D2C commerce on shop.miror.in and marketplaces, and category expansion

**Grow the ecosystem**

*   Scale our services business — the HRT Centre of Excellence, teleconsultations, the doctor network, and corporate programs like Workplace with Miror
*   Deepen the community of 80,000+ women and convert engagement into lasting health outcomes and durable revenue

**Build partnerships**

*   Forge partnerships with healthcare institutions, research bodies, retail and pharmacy chains, and global wellness organizations
*   Represent Miror with media, policymakers, and the broader FemTech ecosystem

**Lead the team**

*   Build, lead, and develop a high-performing leadership team across product, engineering, nutra, marketing, operations, and clinical
*   Set the culture for execution, empathy, and impact

What we're looking for

*   **Proven operating experience** as a CEO, COO, or business head at a consumer healthtech, D2C, nutraceutical, wellness, or consumer-tech company
*   **Track record of running a multi-product business** — ideally one that combines a digital/tech product with a physical product (supplements, FMCG, devices, or consumer health)
*   Strong command of **business strategy, operations, P&L management, and growth leadership** in a bootstrapped or capital-efficient environment
*   Deep understanding of **women's health, healthcare ecosystems, or community-led consumer models**
*   **Familiarity with the nutraceutical category** in India — formulation, contract manufacturing, FSSAI/AYUSH compliance, D2C and marketplace dynamics
*   Ability to partner closely with a Founder/Chairperson — owning execution while honouring vision
*   Empathy, resilience, and the ability to translate purpose into measurable impact

What we value

At Miror, we're driven by empathy, integrity, and inclusivity. We believe that leadership knows no gender — what matters most is your ability to lead with conviction, compassion, and clarity of vision. If you're passionate about transforming women's health and ready to lead a movement that's breaking barriers across science, technology, and wellness — we want to meet you.

Why join Miror

*   Be the operating leader of a pioneering FemTech company shaping the global conversation on menopause and midlife health
*   Build at the intersection of **technology and nutraceuticals** — two of the most exciting categories in consumer health today
*   Work alongside a visionary founder, leading doctors, researchers, and policymakers across India and beyond
*   Join a passionate, impact-driven team that builds change through compassion and innovation
*   Make real social and health impact, at scale, in a business that's already profitable and growing

title: Associate Director Marketing
description:
**Associate Director- Marketing**

  

**About Urban Company**

Urban Company is a technology platform offering customers a variety of services at home. Customers use our platform to book services such as beauty treatments, haircuts, massage therapy, cleaning, plumbing, carpentry, appliance repair, painting etc., all delivered in the comfort of their home and at a time of their choosing. We promise our customers a high-quality, standardized and reliable service experience. To fulfill this promise, we work closely with our hand-picked service partners, enabling them with technology, training, products, tools, financing, insurance, and brand, helping them succeed and deliver on this promise.

  

Urban Company started out as UrbanClap in Nov 2014, when its founders, Abhiraj, Raghav and Varun realized that the home services industry was predominantly unorganized, fragmented and offline. Customers found it difficult to avail quality services in a convenient manner, and service professionals were struggling to make ends meet, having to rely upon several middlemen, contractors, and local aggregators. The founders felt that the industry was ripe for disruption, and launched Urban Company, with three key principles in mind -

*   Customer love: Build a platform that offers truly delightful and differentiated services
*   Partner empowerment: Build a deep, full-stack partnership with service partners, helping improve their earnings and overall livelihood
*   Technology first: Bring innovation and technology to an age-old industry.

  

**What will you do?**

As a leader for Cleaning & Pest Control, you will be the "CEO of Demand" for our largest and most complex business cluster, covering Bathroom & Kitchen Cleaning, Full Home Deep Cleaning, and Pest Control. Your mandate is to move the needle on top-line growth while building a brand that customers trust for their most intimate home needs.

  

**Key Responsibilities**

1\. **Consumer Insight & Empathy**: Go beyond the data. Spend time with customers and service partners to understand the "why" behind the "what." Identify the triggers and barriers for professional cleaning versus the unorganized sector.

  

2\. **Growth Charter & Problem Identification**: Take a first-principles approach to identify why a category isn't growing faster. Is it a frequency issue? A trust deficit? You will define the 6-12 month roadmap to unlock exponential growth.

  

3\. **Full-Stack Brand Marketing**: Lead the creative and media strategy for the cleaning cluster. Whether it’s a massive TVC or a hyper-targeted digital "InstaHelp" campaign, you will ensure the brand remains salient and "top of mind."

  

4\. **Pricing & Revenue Strategy**: Own the P&L levers. You will design pricing experiments, loyalty constructs, and promotional guardrails to optimize for both market share and unit economics.

  

**Who Should Apply?**

● You have experience in brand/content management, growth in start ups, or top-tier consulting.

● You get a kick out of building things and growing them, you aren’t afraid of ambiguity.

● You are comfortable with data analytics and data visualization but can also write a compelling creative brief that inspires an agency.

● First-Principles Thinker: You don't do things because "that's how it's done." You question every assumption until you find the most efficient path to growth.

● Cultural Fit: You thrive in high-intensity environments, value radical candor, and have a deep sense of ownership.

  

**What Can You Expect?**

● Unmatched Ownership: You are the primary driver of a multi-hundred crore business vertical.

● High-Growth Environment: A seat on a rocket ship that is scaling globally.

● Peer Excellence: Work alongside some of the sharpest minds from IIMs, ISB, and top global firms. ● Impact: The opportunity to build a brand that changes the lives of thousands of service professionals and millions of households.

description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.  
  
We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.  
  
Join EY and help to build a better working world.  
  
**Brand, Marketing and Communications Leader, EY GDS Consulting (Assistant Director)  
  
**Are you a strategic communications advisor and a marketer? We’re looking for an Assistant Director in our Brand, Marketing and Communications (BMC) Consulting team to support the marketing and internal engagement experience — to define and execute impactful and compelling strategies, plans and materials to communicate to employees, potential employees and EY client service teams.  
  
**The opportunity  
  
**The individual would be responsible for defining and executing impactful and compelling strategies, plans and materials to communicate to employees, potential employees and EY Core Business Service teams. We work hand in hand with the business to build awareness of our services and solutions; to bring our purpose — building a better working world — to life for our people, clients and communities; to engage our people and build a high performing culture; and to build the commercial value and emotional resonance of the EY and the EY Global Delivery Services (GDS) brand.  
  
**Key Responsibilities  
  
**

*   Leading strategic communications and stakeholder engagement to elevate the visibility and impact of GDS services while strengthening the EY brand across all touchpoints.
*   Strong project management and execution capabilities, managing multiple strategic projects together.
*   Translate complex business strategies into clear, measurable communication plans that drive alignment and deliver tangible results aligned with organizational goals.
*   Be a trusted advisor to the Technology and Business Consulting teams, providing expert guidance on navigating their strategic priorities through effective communication.
*   Champion collaboration by supporting GDS BMC Client Service initiatives and working closely with peers to uphold the highest standards of quality and delivery excellence.
*   Own the management of GDS communication channels, ensuring content is relevant, timely, and engaging across all internal EY and GDS platforms.
*   Lead the seamless coordination of marketing communications efforts, partnering with in-house designers, external vendors, and editorial teams to produce compelling and consistent messaging.
*   Develop and execute innovative, comprehensive communications strategies and campaigns that leverage the power of corporate storytelling to inspire and connect with diverse audiences.  
      
    

**Skills And Attributes For Success  
  
**

*   Strong experience managing and advising senior stakeholders
*   Ability to collaborate, team and network across a matrix organization, understanding how to communication across different cultures, languages, time zones and ranks
*   Proven capability to translate the business agenda into high-quality, relevant, audience-centric communications
*   Ability to work in complex change management environment and support global transformation programs within a multinational environment
*   Excellent project management, problem-solving and delegation abilities, able to manage complex global processes and programs
*   Exceptional attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to multi-task, prioritize, manage to deadlines and work under tight deadlines  
      
    

**To qualify for the role, you must have  
  
**

*   10-14 years in marketing communications or internal communications roles
*   Bachelor's or master's degree in marketing, journalism, communications or public relations
*   Excellent verbal and written communication skills
*   Experience with professional services firm, IT or technology organisation is preferred
*   Experience leading and managing corporate communications teams
*   Experience in a senior communications role for a global and/or complex organization  
      
    

**Ideally, you’ll also have  
  
**

*   Previous experience in large, matrix organizations  
      
    

**What We Look For  
  
**

*   Motivated self-starter who thrives on being a trusted advisor to leadership and on implementing impactful communication strategies, keep reading
*   Excellent leader, a strategic thinker, confident writer and editor, and a strong relationship builder to promote our services within EY and help build the EY brand in the market
*   Advisor for leadership to provide ongoing strategic communications counsel and coaching on brand, marketing and communications matters
*   Ability to develop measurement and evaluation tools, using metrics to inform communications and channel decisions  
      
    

**What We Offer  
  
**EY Global Delivery Services (GDS) is a dynamic and truly global delivery network. We work across six locations – Argentina, China, India, the Philippines, Poland and the UK – and with teams from all EY service lines, geographies and sectors, playing a vital role in the delivery of the EY growth strategy. From accountants to coders to advisory consultants, we offer a wide variety of fulfilling career opportunities that span all business disciplines. In GDS, you will collaborate with EY teams on exciting projects and work with well-known brands from across the globe. We’ll introduce you to an ever-expanding ecosystem of people, learning, skills and insights that will stay with you throughout your career.  
  

*   Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next.
*   Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way.
*   Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs.
*   Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.  
      
    

**EY | Building a better working world  
  
**EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.  
  
Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.  
  
EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

title: Head of Marketing
description:
**Position Title: Lead, Roundglass Giving**

**Location: Mohali, (Hybrid)**

**Position Type: Regular, Full Time Employee**

  

**Who we are:**

**Roundglass** is a wholistic wellbeing company working to empower healthier lives, stronger communities, and a more sustainable world. That _w_ in _wholistic_ is there for a reason: We believe true wellbeing spans the whole human experience, from how we care for ourselves to our connections with others and the planet.

  

This philosophy shapes both what we build and how we work. We’ve created an environment where supporting people’s whole selves fuels their most meaningful work.

  

Roundglass creates real-world impact through two core pillars:

  

Through the **Roundglass Living** platform, we deliver personalized wellbeing guidance rooted in science. The Roundglass Living app helps people develop habits that last through breathwork, meditation, movement, nutrition, music, and more.

  

**Roundglass Giving** is our nonprofit arm, driving our philanthropic vision, including a collection of initiatives championing youth development, women’s equity, and environmental stewardship. We’re on a mission to transform communities by tackling systemic challenges and unlocking human potential.

  

At Roundglass, we're driven by the purpose of making wellbeing accessible and actionable for everyone, everywhere. This sense of purpose fuels how we approach our work and support each other every day. If you want to be part of building real solutions and thrive in a culture where your whole self is valued, we'd love to meet you.

  

**About the role:**

Roundglass Foundation is seeking a passionate and skilled Lead, Marketing (Deptt Head) to join our team. This role is important for our mission by enhancing our visibility and growing our supporter base. This position will work closely with the senior leadership of the Foundation. The Lead Marketing Manager will be responsible for developing, implementing, and overseeing the marketing strategy for Roundglass Foundation. This role requires a creative and strategic thinker with a proven track record in marketing management, particularly within the nonprofit sector. The successful candidate will lead a small team, manage relationships with external partners, and work closely with other departments to ensure cohesive and effective communication of our mission and programs.

  

**What you’ll do:**

*   Develop a comprehensive marketing strategy to increase brand awareness, donor engagement, and fundraising outcomes.
*   Oversee marketing activities including digital marketing, social media management, and email campaigns. This role will also work closely with studio team (responsible for creative video content) and also with the PR team.
*   Lead, mentor, and manage a small marketing team, ensuring high performance and professional development.
*   Collaborate with the fundraising team to create compelling campaigns and materials that drive donor engagement and support.
*   Manage the marketing budget, ensuring efficient and effective use of resources.
*   Analyze and report on the effectiveness of marketing campaigns, using data to inform future strategies.
*   Maintain and develop relationships with media contacts, influencers, and other key stakeholders. • Ensure all marketing materials and communications are consistent with the RGF’s brand and messaging guidelines.
*   Stay updated on the latest marketing trends, tools, and best practices, applying this knowledge to enhance the charity's marketing efforts.

  

**Skills & Qualifications:**

*   Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, or a related field.
*   Minimum of 10 years of experience in marketing, with at least 2 years in a leadership role.
*   Proven experience in the nonprofit sector is highly desirable.
*   Strong understanding of digital marketing tools and platforms, including social media, email marketing, and content management systems.
*   Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
*   Demonstrated ability to lead and inspire a team.
*   Creative thinker with strong problem-solving skills.
*   Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work well under pressure.
*   Proficiency in using marketing analytics tools to measure and report on campaign performance.
*   Preferred candidate from Marketing Agency.

title: Director of Digital Marketing
description:
The Director/ Manager of Digital Marketing will be responsible in assisting the Commercial Director with all Marketing Planning & Execution strategies for the hotel.  
  
**What will I be doing?  
  
**RESPONSIBILTIES MARKETING:  
  

*   To manage and direct all paid advertising, delivering a cost effective and appropriate annual advertising strategy within the agreed budget.
*   To manage all on-line activities for Hilton Mumbai International Airport (HMIA) on Brand.com and Third party websites, ensuring all special promotions are loaded and available with the appropriate lead-in times and that all information is kept up to date.
*   Develop an annual Marketing Plan in conjunction with the Sales/Revenue depts., ensuring that the Plan reflects the strategy needed to achieve targeted results in all revenue streams.
*   Specify market requirements for current and future gap periods/low demand times by conducting marketing research to support the marketing strategies.
*   Ensure that all planned strategies are measurable and are evaluated to gauge their success, with appropriate lead-in times and deadlines.
*   To analyses potential partner relationships for HMIA that will benefit both parties and reach the joint target markets.
*   To manage all aspects of print production, receipt and distribution with effective use of flyers, e-marketing, text campaigns and all other forms of communication.
*   To proof-read and vet all written materials for external customer use, ensuring a high quality is achieved.
*   To ensure strict compliance to Hilton Hotels brand standards in all artwork/graphic design/logos etc.
*   Oversee the consistency of all hotel communication tools in terms of design, copy, color and adherence to Hilton Hotels brand standards.
*   Utilize information on HMIA key source markets and determine appropriate marketing campaigns to increase business from these key markets.
*   Manage relations with relevant local media and ensure a good working relationship is achieved and on-going.
*   Provide direction and follow-up to the PR/Marketing coordinator and other team members relating to marketing.
*   Attend Marketing briefings with updates on campaigns
*   Ensure there is a good working relationship between PR/Events/Graphic team working together with coherent strategies that benefit the hotel.
*   Attend F&B meeting and Revenue meeting to understand the key markets and needs that marketing and advertising can address to improve results. Provide a Campaign Plan detailing activities to be undertaken to ensure results are achieved.
*   Ensure that a monthly report is submitted with copies of all advertising placed in external publications.
*   Submit monthly report summarizing activities carried out previous month and results gained: Month End MIS, Owner’s PPT, Social Media Performance, PMP Report, any other report that may be asked.
*   Work alongside the PR and Events team to ensure that all HMIA events are marketed to ensure maximum publicity and revenue, staying within budget constraints.  
      
      
    

COMMITS TO FINANCIAL RESULTS:  
  
To display a commercial awareness of the market place in which an individual works. An ability to continuously priorities one’s actions so as to deliver the best sales and profit outcomes for the business. Ability to achieve set goals and financial targets. A concern for working toward a common result and committing to excel in attaining all key business objectives.  
  
PLANNING:  
  
Ability to priorities and manage own personal time efficiently within tight schedules. Looks ahead and makes forecasts concerning market trends to set demanding but realistic targets.  
  
INITIATIVE:  
  
The ability to do more than is required or expected in the job to improve results, find new opportunities, exceed in achievement of business objectives and avoid problems through proactively anticipating them.  
  
INTERPERSONAL UNDERSTANDING:  
  
Able to read and interpret both verbal and non-verbal feelings and concerns of others.  
  
RELATIONSHIP BUILDING:  
  
Ability to network, build and maintain friendly contacts with people who can impact on business positively and help achieve work related goals.  
  
CUSTOMER SERVICE ORIENTATION:  
  
Ability to deliver high levels of customer service by focusing on the needs of client and exceeding their expectations.  
  
NEGOTIATION SKILLS:  
  

*   Ability to persuade, convince and influence others.
*   To have patience and take setbacks positively. Able to present information logically and confidently verbally and in written form.  
      
      
    

SELF CONFIDENCE:  
  
A belief in one’s ability to accomplish a task. Confidence in ones decisions or opinions and able to manage the many outcomes as a result of failure to gain a sale.  
  
**What are we looking for?  
  
**Innovative, action oriented individual able to identify and implement results oriented marketing strategies for the hotel.  
  
**What will it be like to work for Hilton?  
  
**Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full-service hotels and resorts to extended-stay suites and mid-priced hotels. For nearly a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands. Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day. And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!  
  
**Work Locations  
  
**Hilton Mumbai International Airport Hotel  
  
**Schedule  
  
**Full-time  
  
**Brand  
  
**Hilton Hotels & Resorts  
  
**Job  
  
**Sales and Marketing

description:

**Experience: 10+ yrs  
  
Location: Bengaluru  
  
Job Type: full-time  
  
We are looking for a strategic and visionary Head of Marketing to lead brand growth, market positioning, and customer engagement for a premium luxury-focused business. This role requires a dynamic marketing leader who can build powerful brand narratives, drive integrated marketing campaigns, and create high-impact visibility across digital and offline channels.  
  
The ideal candidate should bring deep expertise in luxury branding, strategic marketing, and consumer engagement while managing high-performing teams and executing innovative campaigns that elevate brand perception and business growth. This is an opportunity to shape the marketing vision of a fast-growing organization and lead end-to-end brand strategy in a highly competitive market.  
  
The role demands a blend of strategic thinking, creativity, business acumen, and execution excellence. You will work closely with leadership teams to define positioning strategies, launch impactful campaigns, and strengthen the brand's presence across multiple touchpoints.  
  
**Requirements  
  
****Key Responsibilities  
  
**

*   Develop and execute a comprehensive marketing strategy aligned with overall business goals and growth objectives
*   Build and strengthen premium brand positioning across luxury and high-value customer segments
*   Lead integrated marketing campaigns across digital, social media, offline activations, events, PR, and partnerships
*   Drive brand storytelling and ensure consistency in communication, visual identity, and customer experience

**What Makes You a Great Fit  
  
**

*   Creative thinker with a strong eye for branding, storytelling, and customer engagement
*   Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with strong ownership and execution focus
*   Strong leadership presence with a strategic and business-oriented approach to marketing growth

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
**Chief Executive Officer (CEO)**

Industry

Copper Manufacturing / Continuous Casting & Rolling (CCR) / Non-Ferrous Metals

Project Type

Greenfield Manufacturing Project

Location

Rajasthan / NCR Region, India

Role Summary

The CEO will be responsible for:

*   Setting up and operationalizing the **greenfield CCR** plant
*   Building leadership and operational teams from scratch
*   Establishing systems, SOPs, and organizational structure
*   Driving commercial, operational, and strategic execution
*   Creating a scalable and process-driven manufacturing culture

The role requires a highly entrepreneurial manufacturing leader with strong execution capabilities and prior experience in building or scaling industrial projects.

Key Responsibilities

1\. Greenfield Project Leadership

*   Lead the complete execution and operationalization of the copper CCR project.
*   Coordinate with project consultants, machinery suppliers, contractors, and internal stakeholders.
*   Ensure timely commissioning and stabilization of plant operations.
*   Establish operational readiness before commercial production.

2\. Team Building & Talent Acquisition

*   Build the organization structure from ground zero.
*   Hire key leadership personnel across production, maintenance, quality, procurement, finance, HR, commercial, and logistics.
*   Develop strong second-line leadership and succession planning.
*   Create a high-performance and ownership-driven culture.

3\. Manufacturing & Operations

*   Oversee complete plant operations including melting, refining, CCR operations, rolling mill operations, quality control, dispatch, and planning.
*   Ensure production efficiency, yield optimization, and quality consistency.
*   Establish SOP-based manufacturing systems with minimal operator dependency.

4\. Automation & Process Systems

*   Develop a technology-driven manufacturing environment.
*   Reduce labour dependency through automation, process controls, digital monitoring systems, and SOP implementation.
*   Implement ERP/MIS systems for operational visibility and control.
*   Build process-driven operations instead of person-dependent systems.

5\. Commercial & Financial Management

*   Drive profitability, operational efficiency, and cost optimization.
*   Manage P&L, budgeting, working capital, and cash flow.
*   Develop procurement strategies for raw materials and consumables.
*   Optimize production costs, power consumption, and operational efficiencies.

6\. Strategy & Business Development

*   Support market development and strategic customer relationships.
*   Build credibility with cable manufacturers, industrial buyers, and infrastructure clients.
*   Develop scalable growth strategies aligned with future industry demand.

7\. Compliance & Governance

*   Ensure adherence to pollution control norms, environmental compliance, safety standards, and statutory regulations.
*   Coordinate with regulatory bodies and external agencies as required.

8\. Raw Material Sourcing & Strategic Supply Development

*   Develop and secure long-term sourcing relationships for imported copper scrap, copper cathodes, and copper anodes from international suppliers.
*   Build reliable global supply chains ensuring continuity, quality consistency, and competitive commercial terms.
*   Negotiate long-term contracts and strategic procurement arrangements with overseas suppliers and trading houses.
*   Develop sourcing networks across key international markets.
*   Manage procurement risks related to copper price volatility, forex fluctuations, shipment delays, quality variations, and logistics disruptions.
*   Coordinate with logistics, CHA, shipping lines, and finance teams for smooth import operations.
*   Maintain strong understanding of global copper market dynamics, LME trends, scrap spreads, and supply-demand movements.

Candidate Profile

Preferred Industry Background

*   Copper manufacturing
*   CCR plants
*   Non-ferrous metals
*   Aluminium rolling/casting
*   Steel/process manufacturing
*   Wire & cable industry
*   Industrial manufacturing

Candidates with prior greenfield project exposure will be strongly preferred.

Required Experience

*   15–25 years of relevant manufacturing experience.
*   Minimum 5–10 years in senior leadership/P&L role.
*   Proven experience in greenfield plant setup, plant commissioning, manufacturing scale-up, and team building from scratch.
*   Strong operational and commercial exposure.
*   Strong exposure to imported raw material procurement for copper/non-ferrous industry.
*   Existing international supplier network in copper scrap/anodes/cathodes preferred.
*   Experience handling import-based supply chains and commodity procurement.

Key Competencies

Operational

*   CCR operations
*   Manufacturing excellence
*   Process optimization
*   Maintenance systems
*   Production planning
*   Quality systems

Leadership

*   Team building
*   Hiring leadership talent
*   Organizational development
*   Culture building
*   Change management

Commercial

*   P&L management
*   Working capital management
*   Procurement strategy
*   Cost optimization

Commodity & Supply Chain

*   International sourcing
*   Commodity procurement
*   Import logistics
*   Supplier negotiations
*   LME-linked commercial understanding
*   Forex and commodity risk awareness
*   Strategic vendor development

Systems & Technology

*   Automation orientation
*   ERP implementation
*   KPI-driven operations
*   SOP development
*   Process standardization

Personality Traits

*   Highly execution-oriented
*   Entrepreneurial
*   Operationally hands-on
*   Capable of building teams from scratch
*   Process-driven and disciplined
*   Commercially sharp
*   Comfortable working in high-growth industrial environments
*   Capable of balancing strategic thinking with daily operational execution

Education

Preferred

*   B.Tech / BE in Mechanical, Metallurgy, Electrical, Production, Industrial Engineering or related field
*   MBA preferred

Compensation

*   Competitive fixed compensation
*   Performance-linked incentives
*   Long-term leadership opportunity
*   ESOP/profit-linked structure may be considered for exceptional candidates

Ideal Outcome Expected from CEO (3-Year Vision)

*   Successfully commissioning and stabilizing the CCR project
*   Building a strong professional leadership team
*   Creating scalable manufacturing systems
*   Reducing labour dependency
*   Implementing technology-driven operations
*   Building a professionally managed organization
*   Establishing a credible and scalable copper manufacturing brand in India

title: Associate Director, Digital Marketing – Website, User Experience & Search
description:
India, Hyderabad  
  
**R75900** **Associate Director, Digital Marketing – Website, User Experience & Search.  
  
****Location: Hyderabad, IN  
  
**This is an office‑first role. Working together in person supports close, real‑time collaboration and team alignment, enabling us to deliver our best work. You will work primarily in the office, with the flexibility to work remotely for 20% of your time (one day per week).  
  
**Job Summary  
  
**The **Associate Director, Digital Marketing – Website, User Experience and Search** defines and manages the global website, search, and digital user experience strategy. The role shapes the user journey from discovery to conversion, operating at the intersection of organic search, UX, AI ‑ driven search, and analytics. Working cross ‑ functionally with Digital Marketing, Web Development, Global Marketing, and Sales teams, the role translates strategy into measurable commercial impact and serves as the internal authority for SEO and analytics agencies. A core mandate is leading the organization’s response to AI ‑ driven search evolution, including generative search and AEO/GEO.  
  
We are looking for an **Associate Director, Digital Marketing – Website, User Experience and Search** to join our team.  
  
**What You Will Get  
  
**

*   An agile career and dynamic working culture.
*   An inclusive and ethical workplace.
*   Compensation programs that recognize high performance.
*   A variety of benefits dependent on role and location.  
      
    

The full list of our global benefits can be found here: https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits  
  
**Key Responsibilities  
  
****Website Strategy and Governance  
  
**

*   Own the website strategy for lonza.com (CDMO), setting direction for information architecture, content structure, governance, and channel integration to drive brand and commercial outcomes.
*   Convert strategy into a focused website roadmap with web development and build team, defining phased delivery and success measures.
*   Own and govern global web standards (taxonomy, URLs, metadata, templates, editorial guidelines) to ensure consistency across business platforms.
*   Serve as the strategic interface between Global Marketing and web development team, aligning technical decisions with UX, search performance, and commercial impact.
*   Drive continuous optimisation through audits and gap analyses across content, technical health, navigation, and conversion.  
      
    

**Search Strategy – SEO and Organic  
  
**

*   Own the global organic search strategy, spanning keyword strategy, content gaps, on‑page optimisation, internal linking, and SERP feature capture.
*   Set SEO‑driven content priorities with Digital Marketing and the platform teams, grounded in search demand, audience intent, and competitive position.
*   Govern technical SEO with web development, covering crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, structured data, and mobile performance.
*   Manage external SEO agency relationship, setting objectives, managing performance, and embedding recommendations into internal roadmaps.
*   Drive competitive search intelligence, tracking rankings, share of voice, and SERP shifts across priority keywords and audiences using AI as appropriate.  
      
    

**AI Search and GEO  
  
**

*   Lead the organisation’s AI search strategy, defining the impact of generative search experiences (e.g. Google AI Overviews, Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude etc.) on visibility, traffic, and content.
*   Define and implement a GEO framework, covering content structures, authority signals, schema, and entity optimisation to improve AI‑driven discovery and citation.
*   Establish measurement for AI search visibility, tracking presence and citation patterns across LLM‑powered search surfaces.
*   Translate AI search insights into action, embedding guidance into content, digital marketing, and technical web workflows.
*   Continuously monitor the AI search landscape, evaluating platforms, algorithm shifts, and research, and advising senior leadership on priorities and investment.  
      
    

**User Experience And Conversion Optimization  
  
**

*   Own the UX strategy, defining experience principles, journey frameworks, and design standards.
*   Lead continuous CRO, using behavioural data, analytics, and testing to remove friction and improve conversion across key journeys.
*   Partner with Digital Marketing to align campaign landing pages with audience intent, messaging, and conversion goals.
*   Ground UX decisions in user insight, conducting and interpreting research and usability testing as needed.  
      
    

**Web Analytics and Performance Reporting  
  
**

*   Own the web analytics strategy, ensuring robust, scalable measurement across GA4, tagging, events, and goals.
*   Lead the analytics agency and performance framework, defining KPIs, dashboards, and reporting that clearly show web health and channel impact.
*   Turn data into action, translating performance insights into prioritized improvements across search, UX, and content tied to commercial outcomes.
*   Own the website analytics and optimization stack, ensuring the right tools are in place to measure, test, and optimize performance.  
      
    

**Cross-Functional Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement  
  
**

*   Act as the primary web and search strategy partner to Digital Marketing leads, aligning campaigns, content, and performance frameworks to web outcomes.
*   Partner with Sales and Global Marketing to ensure web and search strategy supports pipeline priorities, GTM plans, and brand standards.
*   Serve as the internal authority on search, UX and AI search, building capability through strategic guidance and knowledge sharing.
*   Contribute as the SME to digital planning, budget discussions, and agency reviews across website, search, and UX.  
      
    

**Qualifications  
  
****Required  
  
**

*   Bachelor’s degree in marketing, digital, information science, or related field.
*   10+ years’ experience in digital marketing, with deep expertise in SEO, website strategy, and digital experience.
*   Proven SEO leadership, spanning content strategy, technical SEO, performance analysis, and measurable growth in visibility and organic traffic.
*   Strong understanding of AI‑driven search, including hands‑on exposure to generative search platforms (e.g. Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity) and emerging GEO best practices.
*   Solid UX and CRO foundation, including journey design and use of behavioral analytics to inform experience decisions.
*   Web analytics capability, particularly GA4, tag management, and performance dashboards.
*   Experience managing or collaborating with external agencies.
*   Strong cross‑functional collaborator, able to influence marketing, sales, technology, and senior stakeholders in a global environment.
*   Experience in B2B or complex technical environments, with the ability to understand and communicate scientific or technical topics.
*   Excellent written and verbal communication skills and experience working cross-functionally.  
      
    

**Preferred  
  
**

*   Experience in life sciences, biopharma, biotech, or CDMO/CRO environments.
*   Familiarity with enterprise CMS platforms (e.g. Sitecore, Drupal, Adobe Experience Manager).
*   Experience with SEO, testing, and optimisation tools (e.g. Semrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, Optimizely etc.).
*   Working knowledge of structured data, schema, and entity‑based SEO relevant to GEO and AI search.
*   Understanding of WCAG accessibility standards and web governance.
*   Experience with ABM and its implications for web personalisation and conversion.
*   Exposure to web development concepts sufficient to partner credibly with web development teams.
*   Degree or equivalent professional qualification in marketing, digital, or life sciences.  
      
    

**About Lonza  
  
**At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.  
  
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

description:
**Head of Marketing — ELV (Extra‑Low Voltage) Industry**

  

**Position summary**

Lead and own the end-to-end marketing function for an ELV systems business (CCTV, access control, public address, structured cabling, fire detection, building automation, etc.), driving brand, demand, product launches and customer engagement while integrating AI/automation into marketing and GTM activities. The role reports to the **CEO/Chief Growth Officer** and will build a high‑performance marketing organization that supports sales, product and channel partners.

  

*   Minimum 15 years in marketing with leadership experience.
*   Direct experience with **ELV/security/building** automation or related hardware/solutions.
*   **Led at least one full brand or major product launch.**
*   Hands‑on with digital marketing and marketing automation; familiarity with AI use cases for marketing.
*   Strong stakeholder management and partner/channel experience.

  

**Key responsibilities**

  

*   Marketing strategy and leadership: Develop the 3‑ to 5‑year marketing strategy and annual plans aligned to revenue, margin and market‑share targets; set OKRs and own marketing budget and ROI.
*   Brand and new‑product launches: Define brand positioning, value propositions, messaging and visual identity for corporate and product brands; lead GTM planning and execution for new brand or product launches, including launch briefs, channel readiness, collateral, trials and launch metrics.
*   Demand generation and lifecycle: Own full‑funnel demand programs (awareness → consideration → conversion → retention) across direct, channel (distributors/partners) and enterprise accounts; design ABM and field marketing for large accounts.
*   Digital, content and performance: Build and run digital strategy (SEO, SEM, social, content, email, website, webinars, video) and performance marketing to generate qualified leads and measurable pipeline.
*   AI & marketing technology: Identify and deploy AI and automation use cases (lead scoring, predictive demand, personalization, content generation, chatbots, analytics) and own the MarTech stack (CRM, marketing automation, analytics).

*   Field & channel enablement: Create partner programs, sales enablement kits, technical collateral, training and co‑marketing playbooks for VARs, integrators and system integrators.
*   Public relations & industry presence: Manage PR, analyst relations, trade shows, case studies, certifications, and thought leadership to build credibility in target segments.
*   Team building & vendor management: Hire, mentor and scale a multidisciplinary marketing team; manage agencies and vendors to deliver creative, performance and PR services.
*   Measurement & reporting: Set KPIs (brand reach, MQL, SQL, conversion, CAC, LTV, partner-sourced revenue), run analytics dashboards, and report to the exec team with actionable insights.

**Experience & qualifications**

  

*   **Experience:** 10–15+ years in marketing with at least 5 years in a leadership role; experience in ELV, security, building tech, industrial/automation, telecom, or related hardware B2B/B2B2C industry preferred.
*   Launch track record: Proven experience launching new brands or product lines and delivering measurable market adoption.
*   Digital & AI fluency: Hands‑on experience with digital marketing, MarTech stacks and demonstrable projects using AI/ML for marketing (e.g., personalization, predictive models, content automation).
*   Channel & enterprise knowledge: Experience working with channel partners, system integrators and enterprise procurement processes.
*   Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Engineering, Business or similar; MBA preferred.
*   Skills: Strong strategic thinking, data-driven decision making, storytelling and communication; ability to translate technical product features into commercial benefits.
*   Leadership: Experience scaling teams, managing cross‑functional stakeholders and working in matrixed organisations.

description:
**About Us  
  
**We help make the world a healthier place, for more than 60 years and across the world, we have imagined and developed innovative diagnostics solutions to improve patients' and consumers' health.  
  
A family-owned company, bioMérieux has grown to become a world leader in the field of in vitro diagnostics. The entrepreneurial adventure, which began over a century ago, is driven by an unrelenting commitment to improve public health worldwide and making the world a healthier place.  
  
**bioMérieux India  
  
**We are a 100% subsidiary of bioMérieux SA, Marcy L’Etoile, France and have been in the Indian market for more than 25 years. We serve close to 5000+ customers with our diagnostic solutions in clinical microbiology, immunology, molecular biology and industrial microbiology; providing our healthcare professionals and our industry players crucial information in antimicrobial resistance, sepsis, acute care syndromic approach and food safety to protect patient and consumer health.  
  
Joining bioMérieux, is choosing to join an innovative company with a long-term vision, committed to the service of public health and carried out with a humanistic corporate culture.  
  
We look for candidates whose motivation is to invest in themselves, in the projects of the future, and to participate in a collective adventure led by a pioneering spirit.  
  
**Position:** Head, Marketing  
  
**Education:** MBA preferred  
  
**Experience:** 15+ years of Marketing experience  
  
**Location:** New Delhi  
  
**Reporting to:** VP & GM, India & Neighbouring Markets  
  
**Primary Purpose And Overall Objective Of The Job  
  
**Within the framework of bioMérieux guidelines & strategy, the position will be responsible for implementing the marketing strategy for the BioMérieux products to maximize India & NM business and managing the marketing team by translating the global message into an appropriate action plan for the India subsidiary. This is a combination of product and strategic marketing management.  
  
**Job Description

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
**About Bharat Managed Services**

Bharat Managed Services is a growing technology services company delivering **Managed IT Services, Managed Security Services, SOC/NOC services, Digital Workplace solutions, Procurement as a Service, Software Services, Infrastructure Support, Field Services, and Digital Transformation solutions**.

With a current annual turnover of approximately **USD 5 million**, the company is entering its next phase of growth. Bharat Managed Services is now looking to expand into global markets, strengthen recurring revenue, build enterprise client relationships, and become a trusted international provider of managed technology and cybersecurity services.

We are seeking a commercially strong, client-facing, and growth-driven **Chief Executive Officer** to lead this transformation.

  

**Role Overview**

The CEO will be responsible for leading Bharat Managed Services through its next stage of growth, with a clear mandate to deliver **3X revenue growth within 3 years**, scaling the business from approximately **USD 5 million to USD 15 million annual turnover**.

This is a highly client-facing leadership role. The successful candidate will be expected to personally lead major client engagements, open new markets, build strategic partnerships, strengthen the leadership team, improve profitability, and position Bharat Managed Services as a credible global provider of IT services, managed services, cybersecurity, procurement, software, and digital transformation solutions.

  

Key Growth Mandate

The CEO will be responsible for:

*   Delivering 5**X revenue growth within 3 years**.
*   Scaling annual turnover from approximately **USD 5 million to USD 25 million**.
*   Expanding Bharat Managed Services into global markets.
*   Growing recurring revenue from managed IT and managed security services.
*   Winning new enterprise and mid-market clients.
*   Strengthening SOC, NOC, cybersecurity, procurement, software, and support service offerings.
*   Building strong strategic partnerships with technology vendors, channel partners, and enterprise clients.
*   Improving profitability, EBITDA, operational maturity, and long-term enterprise value.

Key Responsibilities

  

Strategic Leadership

*   Define and execute the company’s long-term vision, strategy, and growth roadmap.
*   Build a clear 3-year business plan to achieve the USD 15 million revenue target.
*   Work closely with the Board, founders, and shareholders to agree business goals, annual targets, budgets, and strategic priorities.
*   Identify new growth opportunities across managed IT, managed security, SOC, NOC, procurement, software services, infrastructure support, cloud support, digital workplace, and enterprise support services.
*   Position Bharat Managed Services as a trusted and scalable global IT services provider.

  

Revenue Growth and Business Development

*   Own the company’s revenue growth strategy and be accountable for achieving annual and 3-year revenue targets.
*   Build a scalable sales engine across direct sales, channel sales, partner-led sales, alliances, and account management.
*   Lead major enterprise sales opportunities, strategic bids, RFPs, contract negotiations, and executive-level client presentations.
*   Drive new client acquisition, client retention, renewals, cross-selling, and upselling.
*   Develop predictable recurring revenue through managed services, cybersecurity operations, service desk, SOC, NOC, infrastructure support, and long-term service contracts.

  

Client-Facing Leadership

*   Act as the senior executive face of Bharat Managed Services.
*   Build trusted relationships with CIOs, CISOs, CTOs, CEOs, COOs, CFOs, procurement heads, and business leaders.
*   Represent the company in client meetings, boardroom presentations, industry events, partner forums, and executive briefings.
*   Understand client challenges around IT operations, cybersecurity, infrastructure, service desk, cloud, digital workplace, procurement, cost optimization, compliance, and business continuity.

Global Market Expansion

*   Develop and execute international market expansion plans across priority regions.
*   Strengthen business presence across markets connected to the company’s UK, US, and India footprint.
*   Identify high-potential countries, industries, client segments, and partner ecosystems.
*   Build region-specific go-to-market strategies for managed services, cybersecurity, procurement, software, and IT consulting.
*   Establish direct sales, partner-led sales, reseller, referral, joint venture, or alliance-based market entry models.

  

Managed IT Services Leadership

*   Scale the company’s Managed IT Services portfolio.
*   Build recurring service offerings across service desk, endpoint support, infrastructure monitoring, network operations, server support, cloud support, backup, disaster recovery, and remote IT operations.
*   Strengthen NOC capability for proactive monitoring, incident detection, response, resolution, and performance management.
*   Develop clear SLAs, KPIs, escalation paths, reporting models, service catalogues, and pricing structures.
*   Ensure managed IT services are profitable, repeatable, measurable, and scalable.

Managed Security Services and Cybersecurity Growth

*   Lead the growth of Bharat Managed Services’ cybersecurity and managed security services portfolio.
*   Expand services such as SOC as a Service, Managed Security Services, Managed Firewall Services, Managed Penetration Testing, vulnerability management, security monitoring, incident response, and compliance support.
*   Build or strengthen 24/7 or extended-hours security monitoring capability where commercially required.
*   Build partnerships with cybersecurity vendors, SIEM, SOAR, EDR, XDR, firewall, identity, endpoint, cloud security, and threat intelligence providers.

  

Procurement, Software, and Support Services

*   Grow Procurement as a Service, including catalogue management, device supply, asset lifecycle, refurbishment, disposal, and vendor management.
*   Build strong relationships with technology distributors, OEMs, hardware providers, software vendors, cloud providers, and logistics partners.
*   Improve procurement workflows, pricing, catalogues, reporting, governance, and margin performance.
*   Support the growth of software services, data solutions, automation, mobile applications, web services, and digital transformation offerings.
*   Strengthen enterprise support services including digital workplace, service desk, field support, IMACD, data center support, connectivity, and infrastructure support.

  

Operational Excellence and Service Delivery

*   Ensure Bharat Managed Services delivers reliable, high-quality, secure, and scalable services.
*   Build operational maturity across service desk, NOC, SOC, field support, project delivery, procurement, software services, and customer success.
*   Improve ITIL-aligned processes for incident, problem, change, asset, configuration, release, and service request management.

  

Financial Leadership and P&L Ownership

*   Own full P&L responsibility for the company.
*   Drive revenue growth while improving profitability, cash flow, and financial discipline.
*   Build annual budgets, forecasts, investment plans, and financial performance dashboards.
*   Monitor gross margin, EBITDA, recurring revenue, contract profitability, utilization, cost of delivery, sales conversion, and working capital.

Sales, Marketing, and Brand Development

*   Build a strong sales and marketing function capable of supporting international growth.
*   Strengthen the company’s brand positioning as a managed services, cybersecurity, procurement, software, and digital transformation partner.
*   Develop strong messaging, proposals, pitch decks, service brochures, pricing templates, and executive presentations.
*   Improve online presence, thought leadership, case studies, webinars, events, partner campaigns, and client success stories.

  

Partnerships and Alliances

*   Build strategic partnerships with technology vendors, cloud providers, cybersecurity platforms, telecom providers, hardware OEMs, distributors, software vendors, data center providers, and system integrators.
*   Develop partner-led sales channels and referral networks.
*   Strengthen vendor certifications, accreditations, and partner status.
*   Negotiate favorable commercial terms, support models, rebates, joint marketing funds, and technical enablement.

  

People, Culture, and Leadership

*   Build a high-performance leadership team across sales, delivery, finance, operations, HR, marketing, technology, cybersecurity, procurement, software services, and customer success.
*   Create a culture of ownership, accountability, customer focus, speed, professionalism, and continuous improvement.
*   Attract, retain, and develop senior talent capable of supporting global growth.
*   Establish clear organizational structure, reporting lines, roles, responsibilities, and decision rights.

  

Governance, Risk, and Compliance

*   Establish strong corporate governance and management reporting.
*   Ensure compliance with legal, regulatory, contractual, financial, cybersecurity, privacy, and data protection requirements across operating markets.
*   Maintain high standards for client data protection, confidentiality, information security, and service integrity.
*   Support certification and compliance initiatives such as ISO 27001, SOC 2 readiness, Cyber Essentials, GDPR readiness, ITIL maturity, or other relevant standards.

  

Required Experience

The ideal candidate should have:

*   15+ years of senior leadership experience in IT services, managed services, cybersecurity, cloud, infrastructure, telecom, digital transformation, software services, procurement services, or technology consulting.
*   Experience as CEO, COO, Managing Director, Country Head, Regional Director, Chief Revenue Officer, General Manager, or senior business unit leader.
*   Proven success growing a B2B technology services business.

title: SVP / VP, Marketing
description:
**About Cvent**

  

Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,500+ employees and ~30,000 customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection.

Headquartered in Tysons Corner, Virginia, Cvent (formerly NYSE: CVT) was acquired by Blackstone and Vista for $4.6B in 2023. Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections. Cvent is redefining the meetings and events industry through technology that advances human connection.

  

**AI at Cvent: Leading the Future**

  

Are you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? At Cvent, we’re committed to continuous learning and adaptation—AI isn’t just a tool for us, it’s part of our DNA. We’re looking for candidates who are eager to evolve alongside technology. If you love to experiment boldly, share your discoveries, and help define best practices for AI-augmented work, you’ll thrive here. Our team values professionals who thoughtfully integrate AI into their daily work, delivering exceptional results while relying on the human judgment and creativity that drive real innovation.

Throughout our interview process, you’ll have the chance to demonstrate how you use AI to learn, iterate, and amplify your impact. If you’re excited to be part of a team that’s leading the way in AI-powered collaboration, we’d love to meet you.

  

**In This Role, You Will:**

  

Cvent is seeking an exceptional senior executive to serve as its Senior Vice President / Vice President, Marketing – India. This role is the senior-most marketing leader in India.

This individual will be a critical force multiplier: ensuring that India’s 200+ marketing professionals are high performing, deeply aligned with global priorities, and operating at the frontier of marketing innovation. Beyond operational stewardship, this executive will serve as a recognized champion of the Event Technology and Martech landscape — representing Cvent’s perspective externally in India & APAC/IMEA region.

The role operates within a global matrix structure: functional direction flows from discipline leaders based across the US, EMEA, and APAC, while this executive holds full ownership of people leadership, operational excellence, talent strategy, and site culture for India.

  

**What you will be doing:**

  

**1\. People Leadership, Coaching & Talent Management**

  

•Serve as the primary people leader for 200+ India-based marketing professionals across multiple disciplines, coaching and developing talent in close partnership with global functional leaders.

•Drive a comprehensive India marketing talent strategy: workforce planning, strategic hiring, structured onboarding, career pathing, and retention — with depth in demand generation, Martech, digital, analytics, events, and creative.

•Identify and accelerate high-potential leaders; build robust succession pipelines and a strong internal bench across all marketing functions.

•Foster a high-performance, inclusive culture aligned with Cvent’s values and spirit of intrapreneurship — where every marketer is empowered to think like an owner and act with urgency.

  

**2\. Champion of Event Technology & the Marketing Stack**

  

•Act as a credible “internal customer”: represent the marketer’s perspective when evaluating Cvent’s own product portfolio and translate those insights into actionable feedback for Product, GTM, and Customer Success teams.

•Champion Event-Led Growth (ELG) thinking across the India organization, ensuring teams deeply understand how Cvent’s platform drives pipeline, revenue, and engagement for enterprise B2B marketers globally.

•Represent India marketing in global forums and industry events as a confident, opinionated voice on modern B2B marketing and Event Technology.

  

**3\. AI Transformation Champion**

  

•Lead Cvent India’s AI transformation within marketing — actively monitoring best-in-class AI tool developments, enterprise adoption trends, and emerging agentic workflows across the global marketing industry.

•Experiment with and evaluate frontier AI platforms — generative content tools, AI-driven video automation (including Goldcast’ s capabilities), intelligent personalization, and analytics automation — bringing informed, hands-on recommendations to the organization.

•Partner with Global Marketing Operations to identify, pilot, and scale high-impact AI and automation use cases in India-based workstreams: content production, QA, campaign operations, and workflow automation.

•Build a culture of AI-first thinking: upskill teams, reduce manual effort, and enable India marketing to operate with the agility and leverage that modern AI tooling provides.

•Translate AI adoption into measurable outcomes — velocity, quality, cost efficiency — and report on impact in global leadership reviews with clarity and precision.

  

**4\. Operational Excellence & Follow-the-Sun Execution**

  

•Own the day-to-day operational rhythm of India-based marketing teams, ensuring reliable delivery against SLAs and commitments to global discipline leaders and regional stakeholders.

•Design and continuously improve follow-the-sun workflows across US–EMEA–APAC time zones for campaigns, events, marketing operations, and creative services — optimizing for speed, quality, and continuity of execution.

•Partner with Global Marketing Operations on capacity planning, prioritization, and skills-to-work alignment, ensuring India bandwidth maps to the highest-impact global initiatives.

•Drive continuous improvement in processes, tooling, and collaboration models — positioning India as a recognized center of operational excellence within global marketing.

  

**5\. Stakeholder Management & Matrix Leadership**

  

•Act as the primary India marketing point of contact for global discipline leaders, regional sales leadership (APAC/IMEA), and executive stakeholders across the business.

•Ensure clear, disciplined alignment of goals, expectations, and performance feedback between India teams and global functional counterparts.

•Partner with HR, Finance, Facilities, and corporate functions in India to champion site culture, employee engagement, and a world-class workplace experience for marketing professionals.

6\. Governance, Brand & Compliance

•Ensure India marketing teams adhere to global policies and standards related to brand, security, data privacy, and event execution.

•Proactively manage operational and people-related risks in partnership with global functional owners.

  

**What you need for this position**

  

•20+ years of progressive B2B marketing leadership within SaaS or enterprise technology organizations, with significant experience in people leadership and operations-focused roles.

•Demonstrated success leading large, multi-disciplinary marketing organizations of 100+ professionals in a global matrix structure, where functional ownership sits with distributed global leaders.

•Deep, genuine familiarity with Event Technology and/or Martech landscape — as a practitioner, platform buyer, or industry advocate. Recognized credibility as a voice in this space.

•Ability to serve as a sophisticated “internal customer”: evaluate marketing technology with a practitioner’s lens and translate insights into actionable product and GTM feedback.

•A proven track record of driving AI adoption in marketing — through tooling, workflow transformation, or team capability-building — with an informed, hands-on orientation toward emerging AI platforms.

•Exceptional coaching, talent management, and recruiting capability: a track record of building high-performing teams, accelerating leaders, and constructing strong succession pipelines.

•Experience managing follow-the-sun or global delivery models, including capacity planning, workload balancing, and cross-time-zone collaboration.

•Outstanding stakeholder management skills — the ability to build trusted, influential partnerships with global CMO-level leaders, regional sales executives, and corporate functions.

•Strong working knowledge of Martech, digital marketing, and data-driven demand generation.

•Exposure to APAC and/or IMEA markets and regional marketing dynamics strongly preferred.

•Executive presence and communication excellence; comfortable representing India marketing credibly in global forums and at industry events.

•Master degree required; MBA or equivalent advanced degree strongly preferred.

  

**Why This Role?**

  

This is a rare opportunity to lead one of the largest marketing organizations in India within a category-defining global technology company at a pivotal inflection point. Cvent is executing the most ambitious transformation in Event Technology history — building a Marketing-first AI-powered event management software that unifies in-person, virtual, and hybrid experiences.

The executive in this role will shape how that transformation is experienced by 200+ marketers across India, establish India as the operational heartbeat of Cvent’s global marketing capability, and help attract and develop the finest marketing talent in the country.
